questions.question: Define statistics.
questions.answer: Statistics is the branch of mathematics concerned with the collection, organisation, presentation, analysis and interpretation of data.

questions.question: What is meant by data?
questions.answer: Data are numerical facts or figures collected for a specific purpose.

questions.question: Define frequency.
questions.answer: Frequency is the number of times an observation occurs in a data set.

questions.question: What is grouped data?
questions.answer: Grouped data is data arranged into class intervals.

questions.question: What is ungrouped data?
questions.answer: Ungrouped data is data presented without class intervals.

questions.question: Define class interval.
questions.answer: A class interval is the range of values in which data is grouped.

questions.question: What is class size?
questions.answer: Class size is the difference between the upper and lower class limits.

questions.question: Define class mark.
questions.answer: Class mark is the mid-point of a class interval.

questions.question: What is mean?
questions.answer: Mean is the average value of a given data set.

questions.question: Define median.
questions.answer: Median is the middle value of an ordered data set.

questions.question: Define mode.
questions.answer: Mode is the value that occurs most frequently in the data.

questions.question: What is cumulative frequency?
questions.answer: Cumulative frequency is the running total of frequencies.

questions.question: What is raw data?
questions.answer: Raw data is the original unorganised data collected.

questions.question: What is a bar graph?
questions.answer: A bar graph represents data using rectangular bars.

questions.question: What is a histogram?
questions.answer: A histogram is a graph used to represent continuous data.

questions.question: What is an ogive?
questions.answer: An ogive is a cumulative frequency curve.

questions.question: What is population?
questions.answer: Population is the complete set of items under study.

questions.question: What is a sample?
questions.answer: A sample is a part of the population selected for study.

questions.question: What is discrete data?
questions.answer: Discrete data consists of distinct, countable values.

questions.question: What is continuous data?
questions.answer: Continuous data can take any value within a range.

questions.question: Define tally marks.
questions.answer: Tally marks are used to count frequencies easily.

questions.question: What is tabulation?
questions.answer: Tabulation is the systematic arrangement of data in tables.

questions.question: What is observation?
questions.answer: Each value in the data set is called an observation.

questions.question: What is statistical enquiry?
questions.answer: Statistical enquiry is the systematic collection of data.

questions.question: What is frequency distribution?
questions.answer: A frequency distribution shows how often values occur.
